title: SMSociety 2026 Conference Programme
snippet: Fabio Giglietto is scheduled as a speaker/panelist for the 'Meta Content Library Community Roundtable: Where to for Social Media Research?' at the 14th International Conference on Social Media & Society (#SMSociety 2026). The panel addresses academic challenges, workarounds, and data access limits following Meta's transition from CrowdTangle to the Meta Content Library.
description: Fabio Giglietto is featured as a panelist for the 'Meta Content Library Community Roundtable' at the 14th International Conference on Social Media & Society, discussing social media research data access.

title: Algoritmi e democrazia, il caso Meta secondo lo studio di UniUrb
snippet: Press coverage focusing on Fabio Giglietto's study analyzing over 2.5 million Facebook posts. The news piece highlights how his findings on Facebook's political content suppression sparked a formal question in the European Parliament on January 13, 2026, and served as the basis for a national television investigation on Rai3's 'Report'.
description: Press coverage of a study by Fabio Giglietto from the University of Urbino analyzing Meta's algorithms and political content suppression on Facebook, which sparked a European Parliament inquiry and a national television investigation.

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: This news article covers a major research study led by Professor Fabio Giglietto from the University of Urbino [2.2.1]. The study reveals how Facebook's algorithmic filters suppressed the reach of mainstream elected officials while failing to curb the visibility of extremist and conspiratorial content, prompting discussions of bipartisan platform regulation in Italy.
description: An article detailing a research study co-authored by Fabio Giglietto on how Meta's algorithmic changes affect the organic reach of political groups in Italy.
